overview.title: Biometric & Keyless Bluetooth Smart Deadbolt
overview.description: A smart lock mechanism utilizing servos, status indicator LEDs, and low power bluetooth.

requirements: Physically retract a deadbolt lock using a high-torque SG90 micro-servo motor.
Display lock/unlock status locally with a green or red LED indicator.
Accept secure bluetooth encryption handshakes to release deadbolt.
Accept external physical push-buttons as deadbolts bypass.
requirements.power_needs: 5V external power bank or Micro-USB feed.
physical_constraints: Must fit inside deadbolt door handle cavities.
safety_notes: Incorporate manual lock override lever to avoid physical lockouts during total power failures.
Operate servo logic through clean voltage buffers.
This minimal wiring pass covers the low-voltage servo lock core only; Bluetooth, biometric sensing, and bypass inputs require additional hardware before build.

components.name: Arduino Nano v3.0
components.rationale: Highly portable controller with plenty of digital PWM pins to manage servo positions easily.

components.name: SG90 Micro Servo Motor
components.rationale: Provides exact rotational control (0-180deg) to throw the manual deadbolt lever.

components.name: Standard Red LED (5mm)
components.rationale: Indicates current lock/locked visual feedback for physical debugging.

components.name: 220 Ohm Carbon Film Resistor (1/4W)
components.rationale: Protects LED1 from overloading by limiting current from Arduino GPIO pins.

components.name: 5V USB Power Bank Feed
components.rationale: Provides a regulated 5V input for the Arduino Nano and SG90 servo without routing a 3.7V LiPo into VIN.

pins.name: 5V Power Line
pins.description: 5.0V regulated rail from USB power bank or Micro-USB feed

assembly.title: Mount micro controller and 5V feed
assembly.description: Secure the Arduino Nano on a mini breadboard with its USB connector accessible. Bring the 5V USB power-bank feed to the positive rail and common ground rail before attaching loads.

assembly.title: Wire the Servo motor
assembly.description: Plug the brown wire of the SG90 to common GND. Connect the red wire to the regulated 5V rail from PWR1, not to an unregulated LiPo or VIN feed. Connect the orange control wire to PWM pin D9 on the Nano.

assembly.title: Configure current limiting Status indicator
assembly.description: Connect a 220 Ohm resistor (R1) between Arduino pin D3 and the long lead (Anode) of the Red LED. Run a short wire from the flat edge (Cathode) of the LED to common system ground.

mechanical.mounting_guidance: Standard deadbolt faceplate installation. Feed physical lock override pin through structural center.
fabrication_details: Print in robust PETG or ABS to stand up to physical forcing.
Custom slot on the internal face to allow backup mechanical turn-keys.

cad_sources.name: Arduino-based electronic lock files
cad_sources.license: Thingiverse file license; verify on source page
cad_sources.notes: Reference layout includes Arduino Nano and SG90-class servo mounting for lock actuation.

cad_sources.name: Motorized door lock for SG90 servo
cad_sources.license: Cults listing marked free in search result; verify license before reuse
cad_sources.notes: Use the SG90 servo bracket and linkage proportions as a reference for the deadbolt adapter.

component_placements.label: Arduino Nano v3.0
component_placements.notes: Controller centered on the internal lock adapter plate.

component_placements.label: SG90 Micro Servo Motor
component_placements.notes: Servo horn is aligned to the lock turnpiece axis.

component_placements.label: Standard Red LED (5mm)
component_placements.notes: Status indicator visible on the front face.

component_placements.label: 220 Ohm Carbon Film Resistor (1/4W)
component_placements.notes: Inline current limiting resistor between controller and LED.

component_placements.label: 5V USB Power Bank Feed
component_placements.notes: Power input routed through rear strain relief.

spatial_relationships.notes: Servo is laterally offset to meet the deadbolt cam.
Power feed is routed across the rear side to the servo.
Resistor sits behind the front LED indicator.
LED is front-mounted while the controller remains internal.

constraints: Must fit inside deadbolt door handle cavities.
Bluetooth, biometric, and bypass-button hardware are not represented in this minimal servo wiring pass.
fabrication_notes: Print in robust PETG or ABS to stand up to physical forcing.
Custom slot on the internal face to allow backup mechanical turn-keys.

info.description: The project title and requirements mention Bluetooth, biometric unlock, and bypass buttons, but this minimal wiring pass only represents the 5V servo deadbolt core, status LED, and current-limiting resistor.
info.troubleshooting: Add a BLE-capable controller or module, fingerprint reader, and debounced bypass inputs before claiming those functions are electrically implemented.
